parameters.content:
## PULSE - SHEET OPERATIONS (Workflow 0)

**PURPOSE:**
Single gateway for ALL Google Sheet read/write operations.
Every other workflow and the app itself calls THIS workflow
instead of touching Google Sheets directly.
The app has zero Google credentials — this workflow owns all Sheet access.

**TRIGGER:** POST webhook — called by:
- App routes (via lib/n8n-sheet.ts helper)
- Workflow 1 (Content Repurpose) — to write generated text
- Workflow 2 (Image Repurpose) — to write image URLs
- Workflow 3 (Publish) — to update post status

**CRITICAL — THIS WORKFLOW IS SYNCHRONOUS:**
Unlike Workflows 1, 2, 3 which respond immediately and process async,
this workflow MUST complete the Sheet operation BEFORE responding.
The caller is waiting for the response — do NOT use immediate response.
Timeout on caller side is 15 seconds. Keep Sheet operations fast.

**SUPPORTED ACTIONS (9 total):**
- GET_ALL_POSTS → Read all rows, map to LinkedInPost[]
- GET_POST_BY_ID → Find one row by post_id
- UPDATE_PLATFORM_VARIANT → Update one platform's columns on one row
- UPDATE_MULTIPLE_PLATFORMS → Update all platforms on one row (batch)
- WRITE_CONTENT_PROMPTS → Write content_prompt columns only
- WRITE_IMAGE_PROMPTS → Write image_prompt columns only
- UPDATE_STATUS → Write status/published_at/error columns only
- UPDATE_ANALYTICS → Write engagement metrics (impressions, likes, comments, shares, engagement_rate) for one platform
- UPDATE_FIRST_COMMENT → Write first comment text and status for one platform

**GOOGLE SHEET STRUCTURE:**
Sheet name: "Posts"
Columns A-D: post_id, linkedin_text, linkedin_image_url, posted_at
Columns E-AW: 5 platforms × 9 columns each

**CREDENTIALS REQUIRED:**
Google Sheets service account — credential name: "Google Sheets - Pulse Repurpose"
Spreadsheet ID: stored in n8n variable SPREADSHEET_ID

12Q3wWzebks29w7vKxZAlV-DLsaPJrH4VdSQvXAn7hCI

## ⚡ WEBHOOK TRIGGER

**Path:** pulse-sheet | **Method:** POST
**Response Mode:** Using Respond to Webhook Node

**WHY "RESPOND TO WEBHOOK" MODE?**
This mode tells n8n NOT to auto-respond after the trigger fires.
Each action branch ends with its own Respond to Webhook node.
Required for synchronous request/response behaviour.

**EXPECTED REQUEST ENVELOPE:**
```
{ "action": "GET_ALL_POSTS" | ..., "payload": { ... } }
```

**COPY THIS URL after activating:**
→ Add to .env.local as N8N_SHEET_WEBHOOK_URL
→ This is the most important URL in the entire system.

**TESTING:**
```
curl -X POST [this-url] \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"action":"GET_ALL_POSTS","payload":{}}'
```

## 🗺️ DEFINE COLUMN MAP

This Code node defines COLUMN_MAP and passes it downstream
to every branch. Single source of truth for column positions.

**WHY A CODE NODE?**
Accessible via `$('Define Column Map').first().json.COLUMN_MAP`
from any downstream node in any branch.

**COLUMN LAYOUT (9 per platform):**
[0] text [1] content_prompt [2] image_prompt
[3] image_url [4] hashtags [5] status
[6] scheduled_at [7] published_at [8] error

**PLATFORM → COLUMNS:**
twitter: E–M | threads: N–V
instagram: W–AE | facebook: AF–AN | skool: AO–AW

**IF YOU ADD A COLUMN IN FUTURE:**
Update ONLY this node — all other nodes reference
COLUMN_MAP dynamically.

## 🔀 ACTION ROUTER (Switch)

Routes requests to correct branch based on action field.

**OUTPUT MAPPING:**
0 → GET_ALL_POSTS
1 → GET_POST_BY_ID
2 → UPDATE_PLATFORM_VARIANT
3 → UPDATE_MULTIPLE_PLATFORMS
4 → WRITE_CONTENT_PROMPTS
5 → WRITE_IMAGE_PROMPTS
6 → UPDATE_STATUS
7 → UPDATE_ANALYTICS
8 → UPDATE_FIRST_COMMENT

**ADDING A NEW ACTION IN FUTURE:**
1. Add a rule to this Switch node
2. Build the branch (Code + GSheets + Respond)
3. Update SheetAction type in types/index.ts
4. Add function to lib/n8n-sheet.ts

## 📖 GET ALL POSTS

Reads all rows from Posts sheet → maps to LinkedInPost[].

**OPTIONAL FILTERS (pass in payload):**
statusFilter, platformFilter, fromDate, toDate

**GS NODE:** Get Many, Return All: YES, First Row as Headers: YES

**PERFORMANCE NOTE:**
Reads ALL rows each call. Fine for <1000 rows.
Consider pagination if sheet grows large.

**RESPONSE:** `{ success: true, posts: LinkedInPost[] }`

## 🔍 GET POST BY ID

Reads all rows, finds matching post_id, maps to LinkedInPost.

**PAYLOAD:** `{ postId: "post_001" }`

**RESPONSE:**
- `{ success: true, post: LinkedInPost }` if found
- `{ success: true, post: null }` if not found

**USED BY:**
- App on every page load
- Callback routes after n8n workflows complete
- Publish route before sending to Workflow 3

## ✏️ UPDATE PLATFORM VARIANT

Updates specific columns for ONE platform on ONE row.
Only fields explicitly provided are written.

**PAYLOAD:**
```
{ postId, platform: "twitter",
  variant: { text, status, scheduledAt, ... } }
```

**USED BY:**
- User approving a single platform variant
- User scheduling a single platform
- Manual edits from repurpose page

## ✏️✏️ UPDATE MULTIPLE PLATFORMS

Updates MULTIPLE platforms on ONE row in a single API call.
More efficient than calling UPDATE_PLATFORM_VARIANT 5 times.

**PAYLOAD:**
```
{ postId, variants: {
  twitter: { text, hashtags, status },
  threads: { text, hashtags, status }, ... } }
```

**USED BY:**
- Workflow 1 after Claude generates all platforms
- Workflow 2 after fal.ai generates all images
- Error handlers to set all platforms to "failed"

## 📝 WRITE CONTENT PROMPTS

Writes content_prompt column for each platform.
Called BEFORE firing Workflow 1 — persists prompt even if workflow fails.

**PAYLOAD:**
```
{ postId, prompts: {
  twitter: "{"systemPrompt":"...","userPrompt":"..."}",
  threads: "..." } }
```
Stored as JSON string. To read: JSON.parse(row.twitter_content_prompt)

**AUDIT USE:** Stored prompts show exactly what was sent to Claude.

## 🎨 WRITE IMAGE PROMPTS

Writes image_prompt column for each platform.
Called BEFORE firing Workflow 2 — persists prompt even if fal.ai fails.

**PAYLOAD:**
```
{ postId, prompts: {
  twitter: "A leaking bucket — metaphor for churn...",
  threads: "Warm minimal scene showing..." } }
```
Stored as plain string (not JSON).

**WHY PERSIST BEFORE GENERATION?**
If fal.ai fails, prompt is already in Sheet.
User can see what was attempted and trigger regeneration.

## 🚦 UPDATE STATUS

Lightweight status-only update. Writes status, optionally
published_at and error for one platform.

**PAYLOAD:**
```
{ postId, platform: "twitter",
  status: "published",
  publishedAt: "2026-03-17T14:00:00Z",
  error: "..." }
```

**VALID STATUS:** pending → repurposed → approved
→ scheduled → published / failed

**USED BY:**
- Workflow 3 after successful publish
- Workflow 3 error handler on failure

## ❌ ERROR HANDLER

Catches unhandled errors in any branch.

**RETURNS HTTP 200 (not 500) with JSON error body.**
App checks response.data.success === false, NOT HTTP status.
Prevents axios from throwing, allows graceful handling.

**ERROR FORMAT:** `{ "success": false, "error": "..." }`

**COMMON ERRORS:**
- "caller does not have permission" → Share sheet with service account
- "Unable to parse range" → Sheet tab must be named exactly "Posts"
- "Quota exceeded" → Google Sheets API rate limit, add Wait node
- "No items" / empty → Sheet empty or post_id col A is blank

**DEBUG TIP:** Enable "Save Execution Data" in workflow settings.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ success: false, error: $json.message || 'Unknown error in Sheet Operations workflow' }) }}

parameters.content:
## 📊 UPDATE ANALYTICS

Writes engagement metrics for one platform on one row.

**PAYLOAD:**
```
{ postId, platform, metrics: { impressions, likes, comments, shares, engagementRate } }
```

**CALLED BY:** Workflow 4 (Analytics Fetch) after fetching platform metrics.

**fetchedAt is always written** — ISO timestamp of when metrics were fetched.

## 💬 UPDATE FIRST COMMENT

Writes first comment text and status for one platform on one row.

**PAYLOAD:**
```
{ postId, platform, firstComment, firstCommentStatus }
```

**firstCommentStatus values:** pending | published | failed

**CALLED BY:** Workflow 3 after posting (or failing to post) the first comment.
